sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 21.11 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.21)

statement.latex: Can some or all groups of the following sorts be written as homomorphic images of nonprincipal ultraproducts of countable families of groups? This is Question~18 in (G.\,M.\,Bergman, Pacific J.~Math., { 274} (2015) 451--495). [r]{(a)} Infinite finitely generated groups of finite exponent. [r]{(b)} For an infinite set$X$, the group of those permutations of$X$that move only finitely many elements. I...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
